'''Tom Holmes''' (born 2 March 1996) is an English [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|sh}} or {{rlp|so}} for [[Doncaster RLFC|Doncaster]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].

He played for the [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]], enjoying loan spells to the [[Batley Bulldogs]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]] and [[Oxford Rugby League|Oxford]] in [[League 1 (rugby league)|League 1]]. He spent the 2018 season at [[Featherstone Rovers|Featherstone]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]], before moving to the [[Huddersfield Giants]] in the [[Super League]], returning to [[Post Office Road]] on loan from [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] in 2019.

==Background==
Holmes was born in [[Castleford]], [[West Yorkshire, England]].

==Personal life==
Holmes was diagnosed with an extremely rare form of [[cancer]] on 5 January 2024, after noticing a rapid decline in health towards the end of last season.<ref>{{cite web |title=Bradford Bulls' Tom Holmes diagnosed with rare form of cancer |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/bradford-bulls-tom-holmes-diagnosed-with-rare-form-of-cancer |publisher=Love Rugby League |access-date=20 October 2024 |date=5 January 2024}}</ref> He recovered from the illness, and resumed playing rugby in April 2024.<ref>{{cite web |title=Bradford Bulls star Tom Holmes back for Widnes Vikings game |url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/news/24281357.bradford-bulls-star-tom-holmes-back-widnes-vikings-game/ |website=Telegraph & Argus |access-date=20 October 2024 |date=26 April 2024}}</ref>

==Career==
===Castleford Tigers===
He joined the [[Castleford Tigers]] from [[Castleford Lock Lane]] ARLFC in 2014 and made his [[Super League]] début in 2015 against the [[Catalans Dragons]]. During three years with the [[Castleford Tigers|Tigers]] he was loaned out to [[League 1 (rugby league)|League 1]] side Oxford RLFC and [[Kingstone Press Championship|Championship]] side [[Batley Bulldogs|Batley]].

===Featherstone Rovers===
On 9 September 2017 it was announced Holmes would leave [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]] for [[Featherstone Rovers]] at the end of the [[Super League XXII|2017 season]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers/castleford-tigers-youngster-holmes-joins-featherstone-rovers-1-8745154|title=Castleford Tigers youngster Holmes joins Featherstone Rovers|work=Yorkshire Evening Post|date=9 September 2017|accessdate=14 December 2017}}</ref>

===Huddersfield Giants===
On 17 December 2020, it was announced that Holmes had left the club to pursue options in the [[Championship (rugby league)|Championship]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/55349250|title=Innes Senior: Wakefield Trinity sign Huddersfield Giants winger on loan for 2021 season|publisher=BBC Sport |accessdate=18 December 2020}}</ref>

===Featherstone Rovers (rejoin)===
On 22 December 2020, it was announced Holmes would return to [[Featherstone Rovers]].<ref>{{cite web|website=loverugbyleague.com|date=22 December 2020|accessdate=7 January 2021|title=Tom Holmes returns to Featherstone|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/tom-holmes-returns-to-featherstone-2/}}</ref>

He played for Featherstone in their 2021 [[Million Pound Game]] defeat against [[Toulouse Olympique]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/12430948/million-pound-game-toulouse-promoted-to-super-league-after-beating-featherstone#:~:text=Toulouse%20beat%20part%2Dtimers%20Featherstone,preferred%20French%20team%20in%202003/|title=Toulouse promoted to Super League after beating Featherstone|publisher=Sky Sports}}</ref>

=== Sheffield Eagles (Loan) ===
In 2022 Holmes joined Championship side Sheffield Eagles on loan in the search of regular first-team minutes.

===Bradford Bulls===
On 11 September 2022, Holmes signed a two-year deal to join Bradford starting in the 2023 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bradfordbulls.co.uk/article/1642/bulls-confirm-holmes-deal/|title=Bradford confirm Holmes deal|website=bradfordbulls.co.uk}}</ref>

On 20 October 2025 it was reported that he had left [[Bradford Bulls]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=20 Oct 2025|author=Ben Olawumi|title=Former Super League trio among quintet released by Bradford Bulls following promotion|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/former-super-league-trio-among-five-released-by-bradford-bulls-ahead-of-2026}}</ref>

===Doncaster RLFC===
On 4 November 2025, it was reported that Holmes had signed a two-year deal to join [[Doncaster RLFC]] in the 2026 season.<ref>{{cite web|date=4 Nov 2025|accessdate=5 Nov 2025|author=Callum Walker|url=https://www.totalrl.com/tom-holmes-signs-for-doncaster-following-bradford-bulls-exit/|title=Tom Holmes signs for Doncaster following Bradford Bulls exit|website=Total RL}}</ref>
